name: Taxonomy Mapping review for Live Biotherapeutics And Engineered Microbes bundle
summary: The staged taxonomy mapping is acceptable but curator-judgment-dependent. PA53 is a strain-defined live microbial intervention and can sit in Live Biotherapeutics and Engineered Microbes, while its microbiota-composition and gut-brain endpoints also overlap Microbiome Composition Modulation. The bundle handles this by cross-tagging the intervention, keeping TBC169 and MTC01 out of the public support map, and avoiding any engineered-microbe or class-wide upgrade. No taxonomy issue should block editorial review.

findings.claim_or_issue: PA53 is mapped as a strain-defined live microbial intervention in this track and also cross-linked to microbiome-composition modulation.
findings.why_it_matters: The same probiotic strain can be interpreted as targeted live-biotherapeutic evidence or as broader composition-modulation evidence; the public support map should avoid double-counting it.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; preserve the cross-track tags and the outlook caveat that PA53 and TWK10 should not generalize to all probiotics or all live biotherapeutics.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged outlook adds PA53 as conventional strain-defined live microbial evidence and keeps engineered-microbe human aging evidence absent.
findings.why_it_matters: PA53 should not strengthen claims about engineered bacteria, SYNB1618-like platforms, or engineered-microbe aging efficacy.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep engineered-microbe claims limited to feasibility and preclinical aging biology until aging-relevant human engineered-microbe results exist.

findings.claim_or_issue: The surveillance session and coverage assessment exclude MTC01 recurrent-C. difficile LBP and TBC169 constipation synbiotic evidence from this public update.
findings.why_it_matters: Disease-specific LBP safety or engraftment and constipation synbiotic symptom evidence would broaden the support map away from the aging-relevant PA53 delta.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep MTC01 as context-only and TBC169 assigned to microbiome-composition modulation unless a future review deliberately cross-links disease-specific platform evidence.
